Feeling adventurous? Zelda wouldnâ€™t mind a stroller stroll, taking in the sights while enjoying being your favorite passenger.If youâ€™re looking for a loving, affectionate companion whoâ€™s ready to be spoiled, Zelda is prepared to step right into your heart (and maybe your stroller).

Bettis gets along well with other cats, but has never met a dog. He has a sweet disposition and a great personality. Bettis is neutered, feline leukemia negative, and his vaccinations are current.
Won't you consider giving this cute cat a forever home?

attributes.descriptionText:
Tara is still learning how to trust the world. She's not the kind of cat who’ll jump into your lap on day one—but give her time, patience, and respect, and you’ll be rewarded with a quiet, loyal companion.

Tara is most comfortable when she has all four paws on the ground. You'll see her confidence grow when she's walking freely around the room. She's a bit defensive when tucked away in a cat tree or bed, so it’s best to let her come to you rather than making the first move. Slow and steady is the way to Tara’s heart.

She would thrive in a home with someone who has experience with shy cats—someone who understands that trust takes time. Once she feels safe, Tara truly enjoys being petted and even seeks it out on her own terms. One of her favorite activities? Chasing the laser pointer! It’s a great way to bond with her while keeping her active and engaged.

Tara doesn’t seem particularly interested in other cats—she’s indifferent, really—so she could do well as a solo cat or in a quiet multi-cat home where she has her own space.

Tara may not be the right fit for every home, but for the right person—someone calm, patient, and kind—she could be an incredibly rewarding companion.
attributes.name: Tara
attributes.searchString: Tara   Black and White Female Medium   Cats Tuxedo / Domestic Medium Hair / Mixed (short coat)s

attributes.descriptionText: Meet Alafair.A gentle soul with a playful sparkle.Alafair is a beautiful, calico domestic shorthair senior with a soft heart and a shy little smile. Sheâ€™s bashful around people at firstâ€”think quiet glances and cautious curiosityâ€”but give her time and sheâ€™ll show you just how sweet she really is.While she may be reserved with humans, Alafair loves the company of other cats and truly shines with a feline friend by her side. And donâ€™t let her senior status fool youâ€”bring out a wand toy and youâ€™ll see her inner kitten come to life!If you have a calm home, a patient heart, and maybe another cat looking for a companion, Alafair could be the perfect, gentle addition.

attributes.descriptionHtml:
Posted 9/10/16, and pictures updated on 11/14/18 and 6/22/21.  Ivana and her siblings, as well as their mother Viola, were at an elderly woman's home in the Bullard, TX area, and needing to be relocated due to allergies and moving.  Ivana and her siblings were caught first on 8/1/16, and had been use to the elderly woman, so they were tame with her.  Ivana is very unique in that she also has some Peach coloring stripes under her front legs, and her stomach along with some Gray spots.  Ivana is still a little skittish of new people, but with one on one attention, she would probably be a good inside cat, but could be outside as well.

D.O.B.:     ? May 5, 2016
Weight:     

Our adoption contracts prohibit declawing of any kittens or cats, it is cruel, can traumatize the pet, and make the pet defenseless if the pet should get outside.  We encourage people to search for kittens and cats already declawed that need to find a new home.

$70 adoption fee will pay for her spay and vaccinations.

attributes.descriptionText: Meet Tucker.  If a sweet older kitty is your preference, he may be a wonderful choice.  This adorable, white-coated (with black accents), golden-eyed boy has his own personality and charm.  He's a bit shy, but, with a little patience, he can be won over with play, treats, or a gentle head-petting.  A forever home would make his life complete. He is fully vetted and tested negative for Feleuk/FIV.  Tucker is being fostered in the Columbia, Md. area.  For more information contact rorrfeline@gmail.com.

Our website is updated daily

Cinnamon is Spayed, UTD on vaccines and tested negative for FIV/FeLV. Approx. DOB is July 15, 2016.

Cinnamon is a sweet, laid back cat who likes quiet company, laser pointers, and treats. She was adopted as a kitten, then came back to NAR as an adult when her people didn't stay together. She will need a patient person to help her feel secure in her new home and allow her personality to shine. 

Cinnamon gets along with other mellow cats. She has never met dogs or children. 

If you're interested in adopting Cinnamon please fill out an application at http://nar.rescuegroups.org.

attributes.descriptionHtml: Wilson!!!!  What a card this dashing boy can be.  He comes from a litter of 9 black and white siblings and he is easily the most adventuresome.  While the others are mellow and laid back content to nap and snuggle, Wilson is out and about trying to find ways to entertain himself.   When we think it is time for him to go back to his room for a snack he likes to play 'catch me if you can!' but when you do he is certainly happy for the snuggles that he gets while you carry him and he will give you sweet kisses knowing that he will soon escape again to find new and exciting adventures....it may just be the laundry room to us but to Wilson it is a world of unknown corners to explore!  Lol!  If you would like a character like this little guy in your life please go to our website at www.emmetcountyanimalshelter.org to fill out the Cat adoption application.

attributes.descriptionText: Sunshine is about  9 1/2 years old as of February, 2026.   She was adopted as a baby kitten and returned to us as a senior through no fault of her own; her mom moved to a home that did not allow pets.  She struggled with the return to rescue yet has now adjusted and is very friendly with her foster mom.  She is timid with new people and will take time to adjust in her new home.  She will do best in a quieter home without a dog and limited cats.  She will need a segregated area to adjust and a home that will be patient and allow her to learn to trust on her own time.  She sure deserves happiness and not being confined in a rescue.  She came back declawed.  Adopt Sunshine today.  www.safehousepets.org.  Approved applications will be contacted within 48 hours.
attributes.name: Sunshine (Declawed)
attributes.searchString: Sunshine (Declawed)   Orange Female  16-0529  Cats Domestic Short Hair (short coat)s

attributes.descriptionText: Petite, spunky, and full of personalityâ€”Ettie is the kind of cat who makes every room brighter. The moment you walk in, sheâ€™s there to greet you, tail high and eyes sparkling, ready for attention (and maybe a snack).Ettie is no ordinary catâ€”sheâ€™s a talented acrobat who loves climbing onto shoulders, zooming around, and even going for stroller rides. Playful and adventurous, sheâ€™s always finding new ways to make life fun.Born with Manx Syndrome, Ettie does need a little extra care: her bladder must be expressed twice daily. But donâ€™t let that scare youâ€”she doesnâ€™t let it slow her down, and the team at Best Friends will happily teach you everything you need to know. What youâ€™ll gain in return is a firecracker of a companion who will fill your days with joy, laughter, and love.💖 Ettie is proof that resilience can be playful, and that every cat deserves a chance to shine. Could you be the one to give her the home sheâ€™s waiting for?
attributes.name: Ettie

Heâ€™s the kind of guy who knows what he likesâ€”and what he likes is good company. Other cats? Big fan. Humans? Clive is well-socialized, easygoing, and wouldnâ€™t even mind cruising around in a stroller walk like the VIP he clearly is.If youâ€™re looking for a sweet senior who brings charm, companionship, and just the right amount of sass, Clive is ready to be your new best friend.

Our website is updated daily

Freya is spayed, UTD on vaccines and tested negative for FIV/FeLV. Approx. DOB is Jan. 2013.

Freya is a sweet girl who just needs someone to understand her — we all know Calico's can have a some of that Tortie-Tude. She was outside for a long time before being brought in to give birth to her kittens. She is friendly, but knows her boundaries. Freya likes to talk and get neck scratches.

She would do best as an only cat in an experienced home. No dogs, no cats. She would do OK with older children that know cat behavior.

If interested in adopting Freya, please fill out an application online at http://nar.rescuegroups.org.

attributes.descriptionHtml:
Please Note: I am in a foster home! Please call 412.321.4625 ext: 228 to make an appointment to see me!

Hi, I''m Pookie! I''m a three-year-old shorthaired tortie who''s looking for my fur-ever home! I came to the shelter because my previous owner could no longer care for me. I have lived with other cats and dogs but I can still be shy around new people. I might do best in a home that''s quiet and has owners who will allow me to slowly gain their trust.

I have been medically evaluated, altered, microchipped, and am up to date on all my age appropriate vaccinations.

My Adoption Package includes:
- Spay/Neuter surgery
- The animal will be provided with age appropriate vaccines UP TO THE TIME OF ADOPTION. As many vaccines are given in a series or require that an animal be of a certain age, this means further vaccines MAY BE REQUIRED AT THE ADOPTERS EXPENSE.
- Feline Leukemia Test
- Deworming and/or fecal exam, Flea Treatment application
- AVID Microchip (form of permanent identification)
- Free Box Cat Carrier
- LIFETIME OF LOVE!!!!

Maise is a sweet and silly girl who's sure to steal your heart. She may need a little time to adjust to her new home, but once she's comfortable, she'll be your purring, playful companion. She loves to observe the world around her and often follows her humans from room to room, curious about what they're up to.

Maisie would do best with older children. She is cat friendly, but has not met dogs.

attributes.descriptionHtml: Hi, I’m Beamer. Four other kittens and I were rescued by Animal Control and luckily came to the SBACC, where we were safe, warm, and well-fed while waiting for our forever homes. I was the smallest of the group and was more scared and shy. I’ve been at the shelter for several years now, and I have a lot of fun zooming around the room, playing, and spending time with my roommates.  I am still nervous around people, and I don’t allow pets or being picked up, but I LOVE other cats. I would need a patient family willing to let me be me, and not expect me to become a people-friendly cat. I would definitely have to have another feline in my home.  My best friends at SBACC are Osaka, Oshkosh, Abbie, and Angelique.  We’re all shy and hang out together, and I would love to go home with one of them.  I’m with Abbie and Oshkosh in one of the pics.

attributes.descriptionText: Hello, my name is Newman. I was found as a stray by someone that cared enough to get me to safety. Being called a stray made me sad. The word stray meant I was homeless and friendless. That was a sad time for me, but things are looking up. I am technically not homeless anymore. It's more like foster care until I am adopted and in a home of my own. I am no longer friendless either. I have lots of roommates that I play and wrestle with. I am a bit timid when I meet new people, but will quickly warm up and purr so you know I enjoy your company. With love and a little time I will gain confidence. Wouldn't you like to be the one to show me just how wonderful life can be?
attributes.name: Newman
attributes.searchString: Newman   Gray and White Male Medium 477  Cats Domestic Short Hair (short coat)s

attributes.descriptionHtml:
=========================
About Our Rescue Group:

The St. Mary's Animal Welfare League is a nonprofit, rescue organization that specializes in the rescue, care and placement of abused, neglected and homeless companion animals. Our animals are vaccinated, microchipped, spayed or neutered and receive medical treatment before being placed in carefully selected homes. We strive to educate the community in areas of humane education and responsible pet ownership as well as helping to control the pet overpopulation in our area by promoting the spaying and neutering of all companion animals.

===Come Meet Our Pets:
Come meet our cats every Saturday from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. located at Petco in California Maryland.
We also have a cat facility in Callaway. The Cat Castle Rescue Center is located in the Callaway Village Shopping Center and is open every Saturday and Sunday from 11am to 3pm.
Please contact info@smawl.org for appointments to meet our dogs.

===Our Adoption Process:
It is the St. Mary’s Animal Welfare League’s policy to attempt to place each of our rescued animals in a home that is well- suited to their needs. Once an animal is placed from our rescue, the animal cannot be sold, given away or otherwise transferred. If the adopter is, at any time, unable to keep or continue to maintain the animal for any reason, the animal must be returned to the St. Mary’s Animal Welfare League.

We work hard at matching our animals to adopters, so they may end their search for a loving, permanent home. Temperament, special needs and personality are all factors in our placements.

===Step #1
The Adoption Application
The initial step in our adoption process is the Adoption Application. This can either be done online or in person. The Adoption form must be filled out completely including vet references and two personal non-family references. Your application will be reviewed and your references will be contacted. We contact your veterinarian to ensure that your current or recently deceased pets were properly taken care of---this means being current on all vaccinations and spayed or neutered. In order to speed up the process, please contact your veterinarian and let them know to expect our call.

===Step #2
Personal Interview You will be interviewed by a SMAWL Board Member or Volunteer either through phone, email or in person. This is an opportunity to learn more specifics about you, your family, your pets and the type of pet you are looking for. At this time, we may suggest that you consider a different animal---one that we may feel might be more acceptable for your situation. If you are completing your application in person, this will most likely be done at that time of completion.

===Step #3
The Home Visit If all goes well with the application verification, we will schedule a home visit. A home visit is required to ensure that your home is everything you say it is on the Adoption Application. It needs to be a suitable safe environment for your new pet. All pets and family members should be home at this time.

===Step #4
Meeting Your Potential Adoptee Once the Application has been reviewed and all references checked, arrangements will be made for you to meet with the animal you wish to adopt, either through the foster parent or the animal’s Case Manager. As a courtesy to our foster families, we do not ask them to show their animals to individuals until we have completed the application approval process. If you have other animals, we recommend a neutral meeting place, such as PETCO or a local park to ensure the animals are compatible outside of their own territory.

===Step #5
Finalizing The Adoption Once the adoption has been approved, we will contact you to finalize the adoption. You will need to sign the contract and pay our adoption fee. (Young Dogs Under 1 =$300, Dogs over 1 = $250, Young Cats 2 years and under =$125, Older Cats over 2 years = $75)
Foster to adopt over a two week period may be available on a case by case basis.

Any applications received for adoptions from beyond St Mary's county line, will be subject to additional terms and conditions available on request.

Please Remember: Our entire organization is staffed by volunteers. We have NO paid staff. Our volunteers must work your application around their business and personal lives. We do our best to respond to all inquiries within 2-3 days, and most of the time we are able to respond within 1 day. Please be patient and thank the volunteers you meet for their service to our community’s homeless pet population.
===
We Serve the Following Cities, Towns, and/or Counties:
We serve the Southern Maryland area.

A home visit is required to ensure that your home is everything you say it is on the Adoption Application. It needs to be a suitable safe environment for your new pet. All pets and family members should be home at this time.===Step #4Meeting Your Potential Adoptee Once the Application has been reviewed and all references checked, arrangements will be made for you to meet with the animal you wish to adopt, either through the foster parent or the animal’s Case Manager. As a courtesy to our foster families, we do not ask them to show their animals to individuals until we have completed the application approval process. If you have other animals, we recommend a neutral meeting place, such as PETCO or a local park to ensure the animals are compatible outside of their own territory.===Step #5Finalizing The Adoption Once the adoption has been approved, we will contact you to finalize the adoption. You will need to sign the contract and pay our adoption fee. (Young Dogs Under 1 =$300, Dogs over 1 = $250, Young Cats 2 years and under =$125, Older Cats over 2 years = $75) Foster to adopt over a two week period may be available on a case by case basis.Any applications received for adoptions from beyond St Mary's county line, will be subject to additional terms and conditions available on request.Please Remember: Our entire organization is staffed by volunteers. We have NO paid staff. Our volunteers must work your application around their business and personal lives. We do our best to respond to all inquiries within 2-3 days, and most of the time we are able to respond within 1 day.  Please be patient and thank the volunteers you meet for their service to our community’s homeless pet population. ===We Serve the Following Cities, Towns, and/or Counties:   We serve the Southern Maryland area.
